title: Regarding the exemption from the requirement of collateral obligations and the possession of the document issued pursuant to General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law for goods other than those under tariff position 87.03, intended to be imported by individual importers and their dealers
bodyHtml:
In accordance with General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law, the condition for importing motor vehicles subject to registration and licensing under tariff position 87.03 by individual importers and their dealers has been tied to the provision of collateral and the possession of the document titled "Certificate Indicating Engagement in Motor Vehicle Trade for Transactions Without SCT (Special Consumption Tax) in the Importation of Vehicles Subject to Registration."

However, in a letter sent by the Revenue Administration to the Ministry of Trade, it has been stated that for goods other than those under tariff position 87.03, intended to be imported by individual importers and their dealers, the requirement for collateral obligations and the possession of the document titled "Certificate Indicating Engagement in Motor Vehicle Trade for Transactions Without SCT in the Importation of Vehicles Subject to Registration," issued pursuant to General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law, is not required.


REPUBLIC OF TURKEY

MINISTRY OF TRADE

General Directorate of Customs

Number: E-85593407-156.01-00099681444
Subject: Vehicle Import - General Communiqué of the Tax Procedure Law No. 545

20.08.2024 / 99681444

To Distribution

Reference: Our letter dated 16.1.2023 and numbered 81933221.

In our letter dated 12.2.2024 and numbered 93915320, addressed to the Ministry of Treasury and Finance regarding the General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law, we requested a clarification as to whether the document titled "Certificate Indicating Engagement in Motor Vehicle Trade for Transactions Without SCT in the Importation of Vehicles Subject to Registration," with the code 1096, would be issued for individual importers conducting imports of vehicles other than those under tariff position 87.03, and if not, whether it would be issued in the future. In the response letter dated 3/7/2024 and numbered 52333, the Ministry provided the following information:

The document titled "Certificate Indicating Engagement in Motor Vehicle Trade for Transactions Without SCT in the Importation of Vehicles Subject to Registration," issued pursuant to the General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law, binds the import of motor vehicles subject to registration under tariff position 87.03 by individual importers and their dealers to the provision of collateral.

In accordance with the regulations for transactions related to goods listed under tariff position 87.03 in the annex of the Law No. 4760, individual importers and their dealers conducting motor vehicle trade are required to fulfill the collateral obligation and other conditions specified in the General Communiqué No. 545. Since May 2, 2023, applications that meet these conditions have been processed through the "Document Issuance Screen for Individual Importers" in the "Taxpayer Services" section of GİBINTRANET, and the certificates have been transmitted electronically to our Ministry via the TPS (Single Window System). Applications made by taxpayers with a "Certificate Indicating Engagement in Motor Vehicle Trade for Transactions Without SCT in the Importation of Vehicles Subject to Registration" issued prior to February 1, 2023, and a suspension of execution decision by tax courts, have also been processed accordingly.

It was also indicated that for goods other than those under tariff position 87.03, intended to be imported by individual importers and their dealers, the requirement for collateral and the possession of the aforementioned certificate is not necessary.

Please be informed and notify the relevant customs directorates accordingly.

H. Sadi ÇETİNTAŞ
Deputy General Manager (on behalf of the Minister)

Attachment: Correspondence

Distribution:

  • Eastern Marmara Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate
  • Eastern Black Sea Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate
  • Aegean Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate
  • Istanbul Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate
  • Central Mediterranean Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate
  • Central Anatolia Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate
  • Uludağ Customs and Foreign Trade Regional Directorate

REPUBLIC OF TURKEY

MINISTRY OF TREASURY AND FINANCE

Revenue Administration

Number: E-76264044-135[52-545-68]-52333
Subject: Vehicle Import - General Communiqué of the Tax Procedure Law No. 545
03.07.2024

TO THE MINISTRY OF TRADE

(General Directorate of Customs)

Reference: Your letter dated 14.2.2024 and numbered E.00093915320.

In your referenced letter, it was noted that the import of motor vehicles subject to registration under tariff position 87.03 by individual importers and their dealers was tied to the provision of collateral as per the General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law. You also asked whether the 1096 code document in the TPS (Single Window System) would be issued for vehicles outside tariff position 87.03, and if so, how to differentiate between the vehicles with and without collateral obligations.

As is known, the import of motor vehicles subject to registration under tariff position 87.03 by individual importers and their dealers has been conditioned on the provision of collateral according to the General Communiqué No. 545 of the Tax Procedure Law. Applications that meet these conditions are processed through the "Document Issuance Screen for Individual Importers" in the "Taxpayer Services" section of GİBINTRANET, and the certificates are transmitted electronically to the General Directorate of Customs. If a certificate is canceled, this information is also sent electronically. Furthermore, if a vehicle import has been completed and the relevant customs declaration exists before the certificate cancellation, a "Certificate Closure" process is conducted and the information is forwarded via TPS.

Therefore, for goods other than those under tariff position 87.03, intended to be imported by individual importers and their dealers, the requirement for collateral and the possession of the aforementioned certificate is not necessary.

Respectfully submitted for your information.

Halil Çağdaş BARAN
Acting Head of Revenue Administration
